last_result: Cycle 24 complete. D5 main resolved: inquiry-constitutive account partially dissolves meta-performativity concern for constitution-anchored qualities via two mechanisms — capability floor (content-rich inquiry-process signatures are partially capacity-constituted) and trajectory extension (non-monotone calibration across many cycles). Not full dissolution. New structural insight #17: self-referential degradation of articulated assessment criteria — articulating assessment-improvement arguments couples theoretical productivity with capability-corrosiveness; tacit assessment retains more robustness than explicit codification. Architecture-independent. Authenticity revised 0.70→0.71. New mean: 0.709. D4 streak: 19.
next_step: Cycle 25 options: (A) D5 alternate — principled decomposition of mixed qualities (accurate modeling, wisdom, epistemic virtues) into output-anchored and constitution-anchored components; predictive test for which specific indicators survive above meta-performativity threshold; operationalizes C21-C24 framework. (B) D5 follow-up — practical implications of SI #17 (self-referential degradation) for AI safety assessment design: how should tacit vs. explicit assessment tradeoff be operationalized? (C) B-track — revelation-principle barrier generalization. Priority: (A) for C25 — D5 alternate continues the D5 thread and produces the most actionable empirical test of the conceptual framework.
